name: deckhouse

metadata.name: deckhouse
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Wrapper for hooks for Deckhouse, Addon Operator, and Shell Operator by Flant

documentation:
# Deckhouse python library

Simplifies writing module hooks for Kubernetes operators:

- [Deckhouse](https://github.com/deckhouse/deckhouse)
- [Addon Operator](https://github.com/flant/addon-operator)
- [Shell Operator](https://github.com/flant/shell-operator) (values are unaccessible)

**NOTE**:
- The API is in alpha stage


## Install

```bash
pip install deckhouse
```

## Sample hook

```python
# hello.py
from deckhouse import hook

def main(ctx: hook.Context):
    # Manipulate kubernetes state
    # ... object = { "kind" : "Pod", "apiVersion" : "v1", ... }
    ctx.kubernetes.create_or_update(object)

    # Export metrics
    # ... metric = { "name" : "power", "group": "my_hook", "set" : 9000, ... }
    ctx.metrics.collect(metric)

    # Use in-memory values for helm chart. Shell Operator does not support values, but Addon Operator and Deckhouse do.
    ctx.values.myModule.deployment.replicas = 5


if __name__ == "__main__":
    hook.run(main, configpath="hello.yaml") # 'config' arg is also supported for raw string
```

```yaml
# hello.yaml
configVersion: v1
onStartup: 10
```

## How to test

An example for pytest

```python
# hello_test.py

from hello import main
from deckhouse import hook

# Inputs
#   initial_values = { ... }
#   binding_context = [ { ... } ]
# Outputs
#   expected_metrics = [ ... ]
#   expected_kube_operations = [ ... ]
#   expected_values_patches = [ ... ]
#   expected_values = { ... }

def test_hello():
    out = hook.testrun(main, binding_context, initial_values)

    assert out.metrics.data == expected_metrics
    assert out.kube_operations.data == expected_kube_operations
    assert out.values_patches.data == expected_values_patches

    assert out.values.myModule.deployment.replicas == 5
```

## Development

- Create virtual environment with python >= 3.8
- [Install poetry](https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installation)
- `poetry install`
- `make test`

classes.name: deckhouse.values.ValuesPatchesCollector
documentation: Wrapper for the values manipulations (JSON patches)

classes.name: deckhouse.values.PatchGenerator

methods.name: deckhouse.values.PatchGenerator.generate
documentation:
Generate JSON patches for the dictdiffer change.

**NOTE**: make sure to pass JSON serizlizable values, e.g. raw dicts, if you use dict
wrappers like DotMap.

Args:
    change (dict): the dictdiffer change

Yields:
    dict: JSON patch dict

methods.name: deckhouse.values.PatchGenerator.__generate
documentation:
Converts dictdiffer change to JSON patches suitable for Addon Operator.
https://jsonpatch.com/#operations

Generates appropriate JSON patches for the dictdiffer changes to be useful in Addon Operator.

Addon Operator does not permit using "replace" operation, so we use "add" instead. And we treat
arrays as whole. We have to remove them and set the new value instead of patching them.

modules.name: deckhouse.utils
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
Copyright 2024 Flant JSC Licensed under Apache License 2.0

classes.name: deckhouse.utils.BaseConversionHook
documentation:
Base class for convertion webhook realisation.
Usage.
Create class realisation with methods which named as kubernetesCustomResourceConversion[*].name and
    which get dict resource for conversion and returns tuple (string|None, dict) with result
    if string is not None conversion webhook will return error.

For example. We have next conversion webhook declaration:
    configVersion: v1
    kubernetesCustomResourceConversion:
    - name: alpha1_to_alpha2
      crdName: nodegroups.deckhouse.io
    conversions:
    - fromVersion: deckhouse.io/v1alpha1
      toVersion: deckhouse.io/v1alpha2

Then we can create next class for this conversion:

    class NodeGroupConversion(ConversionDispatcher):
        def __init__(self, ctx: Context):
            super().__init__(ctx)

        def alpha1_to_alpha2(self, o: dict) -> typing.Tuple[str | None, dict]:
            o["apiVersion"] = "deckhouse.io/v1alpha2"
            return None, o

We added method alpha1_to_alpha2 (named as binding name for conversion), get dict for conversion and returns a tuple.

And in hook file we can use this class in the next way:
    def main(ctx: hook.Context):
        NodeGroupConversion(ctx).run()

    if __name__ == "__main__":
        hook.run(main, config=config)

methods.name: deckhouse.validations.ValidationsCollector.data

The data is a list of ONLY ONE object, because this object will be a single JSON
conversion response.

Returns:
    list: the list of single response

Wrapper for the validating feature of Shell Operator.

https://github.com/flant/shell-operator/blob/main/BINDING_VALIDATING.md

modules.name: deckhouse.tests
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
Copyright 2024 Flant JSC Licensed under Apache License 2.0

functions.name: deckhouse.tests.__assert_validation
documentation: msg: typing.Tuple[str, ...] | str | None

functions.name: deckhouse.tests.assert_validation_allowed
documentation:
Assert that validation webhook returns "allowed" result

Args:
    t (unittest.TestCase): unit test context (self in Test class method)
    o (hook.Output): output from hook.testrun
    msg (any): tuple or str or None, warnings for output, tuple for multiple warnings, str for one warning, None without warnings
msg: typing.Tuple[str, ...] | str | None

functions.name: deckhouse.tests.assert_validation_deny
documentation:
Assert that validation webhook returns "deny" result

Args:
    t (unittest.TestCase): unit test context (self in Test class method)
    o (hook.Output): output from hook.testrun
    msg (str): failed message

functions.name: deckhouse.tests.assert_common_resource_fields
documentation:
Assert for object represented as dict api version name and namespace
This fixture may be useful for conversion webhook tests for checking
    that conversion webhook did not change name and namespace and set valid api version

Args:
    t (unittest.TestCase): unit test context (self in Test class method)
    obj (hook.Output): output from hook.testrun
    api_version (str): API version for expected object
    name (str): name of expected object
    namespace (str): namespace of expected object

functions.name: deckhouse.tests.assert_conversion
documentation:
Assert result of conversion webhook

Args:
    t (unittest.TestCase): unit test context (self in Test class method)
    o (hook.Output): output from hook.testrun
    res (any): Can be: dict - for one resource convertion, list of dicts for conversion multiple objects per request
               or function callable[ (unittest.TestCase, typing.List[dict]) -> None ] for assert objects for your manner
    failed_msg (str | None): should present for asserting failed result of webhook
res: dict | typing.List[dict] | typing.Callable[[unittest.TestCase, typing.List[dict]], None]

modules.name: deckhouse.hook
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
Copyright 2022 Flant JSC Licensed under Apache License 2.0

functions.name: deckhouse.hook.__run
documentation:
Run the hook function with config. Accepts config path or config text.

```
param func

the function to run

param binding_context

the list of hook binding contexts

param config_values

config values

param initial_values

initial values

param module_name

module name in camelCase

return output

output means with all generated payloads and updated values
```

functions.name: deckhouse.hook.run
documentation:
Run the hook function with config. Accepts config path or config text.

```
param configpath

path to the hook config file

param config

hook config text itself
```

functions.name: deckhouse.hook.testrun
documentation:
Test-run the hook function. Accepts binding context and initial values.

Returns output means for metrics, kubernetes, values patches, and also modified values for more
convenient tests.

```
param binding_context

the list of hook binding contexts

param initial_values

initial values

return

output means for metrics and kubernetes
```

classes.name: deckhouse.hook.Output

methods.name: deckhouse.hook.Output.flush
documentation: TODO  logger: --log-proxy-hook-json / LOG_PROXY_HOOK_JSON (default=false)
Delegate hook stdout/stderr JSON logging to the hooks and act as a proxy that adds some extra
fields before just printing the output. NOTE: It ignores LOG_TYPE for the output of the hooks;
expects JSON lines to stdout/stderr from the hooks

Container for output means for metrics, kubernetes, and values.

Metrics, Kubernetes JSON patches and values JSON patches are collected in underlying storages,
whether shell-operator (or addon-operator) file paths, or into memory.

methods.name: deckhouse.kubernetes.KubeOperationCollector.merge_patch

---
param apiVersion: |-

  use preferred apiVersion for the given kind.

  object.
param kind: object Kind.
param name: object name.
param namespace: |-
  object Namespace. If empty, implies operation on a Cluster-level
  resource.
param operation: specifies an operation's type.
param patch: |-
  describes transformations to perform on an object. Can be a normal JSON or
